title: E-Commerce Support Bot
subtitle: Reducing support tickets by 62% with a GPT-4 powered chatbot
problem: ModernRetail Co. was drowning in repetitive customer support tickets — order tracking, return requests, and product questions made up 80% of their volume. Their 4-person support team was spending most of their day on copy-paste responses, leaving complex issues unresolved for days.
approach: Conducted a 2-day audit of their Zendesk history to identify the top 20 most common ticket types
Built a RAG pipeline that ingests their product catalog, return policies, and shipping data in real-time from Shopify
Developed a GPT-4 powered chatbot with custom system prompts tuned to their brand voice
Integrated with Shopify APIs for live order tracking and return initiation
Added a smart escalation system — the bot detects frustration and routes to a human agent automatically

testimonial.quote: Our support ticket volume dropped by 62% within the first month. The chatbot handles everything from order tracking to product questions, and our customers actually prefer it to waiting for a human agent.
testimonial.name: Sarah Chen

title: Legal Document Analyzer
subtitle: AI-powered contract analysis that extracts key clauses and flags risks in seconds
problem: Hartfield & Associates' junior lawyers spent 4-6 hours per contract on manual review — reading through 50+ page documents to extract key clauses, identify risks, and write summaries. The firm was scaling and couldn't hire fast enough to keep up with contract volume.
approach: Analyzed 200+ previously reviewed contracts to understand the firm's review criteria and risk taxonomy
Built a document processing pipeline that handles PDFs, Word docs, and scanned images (OCR)
Used LangChain with GPT-4 to create specialized extraction chains for 15 clause types
Developed a risk scoring algorithm that flags high-risk clauses with explanations
Built a React dashboard that presents extracted data, highlights, and allows lawyer annotations
Integrated with their existing NetDocuments DMS via API

title: Real Estate Lead Qualifier
subtitle: Automated pipeline that scores and routes leads in real-time via Slack
problem: Summit Real Estate Group was receiving 200+ inquiries per week across their website, Zillow, and email. Every inquiry was manually checked by a coordinator who would Google the prospect, assess intent, and assign it to an agent. This took 30+ hours per week and hot leads often sat untouched for hours.
approach: Mapped their existing lead flow from all channels (website forms, Zillow API, email parsing)
Built an automated ingestion pipeline on Make.com that captures leads from all sources into Airtable
Used OpenAI to analyze each lead's message for intent signals, budget indicators, and urgency
Created a scoring algorithm (1-100) based on AI analysis + data enrichment (property type, location, budget range)
Set up automatic Slack notifications — hot leads (80+) ping the assigned agent immediately, warm leads go into a daily digest

testimonial.quote: The automation pipeline saved our team roughly 30 hours per week on lead qualification alone. We went from manually checking every inquiry to having AI-scored leads delivered right to our Slack in real time.
testimonial.name: Marcus Rivera
testimonial.role: Sales Director, Summit Real Estate Group

title: Content Repurposing Engine
subtitle: Turning one blog post into 10+ pieces of content automatically
problem: GrowthPath Digital was producing one long-form blog post per week but struggling to repurpose it across channels. Their content team would manually rewrite the post into tweets, LinkedIn posts, email newsletters, and Instagram captions — a process that took 8+ hours per post and often didn't happen at all.
approach: Analyzed their brand voice across all channels to create detailed tone-of-voice prompts for each platform
Built an n8n workflow triggered whenever a new post is published in Notion
Used Claude API with platform-specific system prompts to generate: 5 tweets/threads, 2 LinkedIn posts, 1 email newsletter, 3 Instagram captions
Each output is drafted in Buffer (social) and their email platform for human review before publishing
Added a feedback loop — the team can rate outputs, and the prompts auto-adjust over time

testimonial.quote: What impressed me most was the strategic thinking. It wasn't just about building a bot — they helped us rethink our entire content workflow. We now produce 4x the content with the same team.
testimonial.name: Anika Patel
